title: prunella vulgaris subsp. hastifolia
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. hastifolia, also known as self-heal or heal-all, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae, native to Europe and Asia, and widely naturalized in other parts of the world. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris subsp. hastifolia include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Prunella vulgaris subsp. hastifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
